checklist.description: Feature is where the user expects it; shortcuts match usage frequency.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Feature placement matches user mental model
• High-frequency actions have shortcuts
• Entry points consistent across home/hub/menu/search

checklist.description: Labels use correct Vietnamese business terminology; avoids confusion between transfer/QR/receive/top-up/withdraw.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Labels match local terminology
• No ambiguity between similar actions (chuyển ví vs chuyển bank vs QR)
• Consistent naming across flows

checklist.description: Search, quick actions, recent actions, and context CTAs exist to help users find features fast.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Search returns relevant results
• Quick actions and recent actions available
• Context CTAs surface at right moments

checklist.description: Features grouped logically; mini-app content not mixed with core payment actions in a way that causes confusion.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Feature categories make intuitive sense
• Mini-app / campaign content clearly separated from core utilities
• No misleading groupings

checklist.description: No duplicate CTAs doing the same thing on one screen; labels consistent; positions don't cause mistaps; no double-submit risk.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Only one primary CTA per key action
• If two CTAs exist, same semantics and consistent labels
• Double-submit guarded (CTA deactivated on submit)
• 'X' / back behavior consistent across flows
checklist.failure_example: Lending: duplicated 'X' buttons. MMF: tappable-looking elements that don't respond. 'Trạm Săn Thưởng' X goes to Bill home; 'Thống kê chi tiêu' X goes to Zalopay home.

checklist.description: No duplicate versions of the same concept floating in different parts of the product (e.g., multiple transaction history views that aren't consistent).
checklist.what_to_check:
• Identify all versions of the same concept
• Content/state synced across versions
• Strategic reason documented if multiple versions exist
• Deep links and notifications go to correct view
checklist.failure_example: LSGD SDSL vs LSGD Zalopay vs lịch sử xu — user may think transaction is missing if they look at wrong history.

checklist.description: Touch targets and spacing large enough to prevent accidental taps, especially at cashier, confirm, and home shortcuts.
checklist.what_to_check:
• No two CTAs placed too close together
• Tap targets meet minimum size guidelines
• Sticky elements don't obscure content
• Swipe/back gestures don't conflict with CTAs
• Acceptable on small-screen devices
checklist.failure_example: OTA home: two tap zones too close, causing chaotic feel for users.

checklist.description: Evaluate completion time not just for best-case but edge cases: slow network, OTP delay, source-of-fund change, missing KYC/PIN, pending reconciliation, return from bank app.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Minimum steps; minimal redundant confirmations
• Clear exit/continue path always available
• Edge cases tested: OTP timeout, network drop, bank callback delay

checklist.description: Reduce manual input effort vs. competitors. Autofill, defaults, scan/contacts support, saved bills.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Autofill and recent suggestions available
• Sensible defaults (amount, source, recipient)
• Scan and contacts supported where relevant
• Saved billers/recipients available
• Compare steps/fields vs. benchmark apps

checklist.description: Navigating from banner/notification/history into a flow preserves context and state.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Context preserved when entering from notification
• State preserved when entering from history
• Deep links land on correct screen

checklist.description: Returning after backgrounding, network loss, OTP timeout, or bank callback still allows resumption.
checklist.what_to_check:
• App backgrounded and resumed: state intact
• Network dropped mid-flow: can resume
• OTP timeout: clear re-entry path
• Bank/partner callback: returns to correct state

checklist.description: User can exit/cancel any flow and feels in control. Back/close behavior is consistent. Exiting during processing directs user to check history rather than re-submit.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Back/close behavior consistent throughout flow
• Cancel/exit option present when appropriate
• Warning only shown when real risk exists (losing voucher ≠ risk)
• Exiting during processing: clear 'check history' guidance
• Returning to flow: resumes correctly or clears state clearly

checklist.description: Cross-sell (banners, service suggestions, voucher upsells) must not interrupt the primary task.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Cross-sell doesn't obscure primary CTA
• Cross-sell can be dismissed
• Cross-sell doesn't reset input/state
• Cross-sell doesn't redirect without intent
• If mandatory: placed post-result or at entry, never at payment decision point

checklist.description: No button requires more than one click or has too small a tap zone. Test across multiple devices and platforms.
checklist.what_to_check:
• All interactive elements respond on single tap
• Tap zones meet minimum size
• Tested on both iOS and Android, small and large screens

checklist.description: Focused on each product's home screen (not just Zalopay home). High-frequency actions prioritized; utility vs. campaign hierarchy clear.
checklist.what_to_check:
• High-frequency actions prominently placed
• Clear hierarchy: utility vs. campaign/cross-sell
• Suggestion modules don't obscure primary CTA
• Re-entry to in-progress tasks (pending, KYC, bill)
• No inadvertent task-switching when user has clear intent

checklist.description: Banners and suggestions support task completion; don't overwhelm core utility.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Banners are relevant and dismissable
• Modules don't dominate above-the-fold real estate
• Campaign content clearly differentiated from utility

checklist.description: Reminders for in-progress tasks: pending bill, incomplete KYC, pending transaction, expiring voucher.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Pending bills surfaced proactively
• Incomplete KYC/onboarding nudged
• Pending transactions visible
• Expiring vouchers/benefits reminded

checklist.description: Content and quick actions adapt to user behavior (new vs. power user).
checklist.what_to_check:
• New users see onboarding-oriented actions
• Power users see their most-used actions
• Irrelevant actions for user segment are deprioritized

checklist.description: New users can complete their first successful transaction with minimal friction. The path from install → KYC → first transaction is intentionally designed.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Onboarding steps are clear and sequenced logically
• KYC requirements explained before user hits a wall
• First transaction flow is the simplest possible
• Drop-off points in activation funnel identified and monitored
checklist.failure_example: User creates account, tries to transfer, hits KYC wall with no explanation of what's needed or how long it takes.

checklist.description: When a feature requires prerequisites (KYC tier, linked bank, PIN setup), these are surfaced before the user invests effort in a flow they can't complete.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Prerequisites shown at entry point, not mid-flow
• Clear upgrade/complete path provided
• Estimated time/effort for prerequisite shown
• Progress saved if user leaves to complete prerequisite
checklist.failure_example: User fills out full bill payment form, hits submit, and only then learns their KYC level doesn't support this transaction type.

checklist.description: Push notifications, SMS, and in-app messages are clear, actionable, and deep-link to the right screen. OTP messages are unambiguous.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Transaction confirmation notifications are specific (amount, recipient, status)
• Deep link from notification goes to correct screen/state
• OTP SMS copy is clear about what it's authorizing
• Risk alert notifications explain what to do next
• Notification volume doesn't cause opt-out fatigue
checklist.failure_example: Ambiguous 'your transaction is being processed' push at 11pm with no deep link. User doesn't know if it succeeded or failed.

checklist.description: Sufficient signals for user to trust they are paying the right person/merchant/QR before confirming.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Recipient name/verified status displayed clearly
• QR source identified
• Merchant verification signal present at cashier

checklist.description: Define high-risk actions for each product (not just account takeover) and document mitigations beyond user authentication.
checklist.what_to_check:
• High-risk actions enumerated per product
• Mitigation mechanisms documented
• Mitigations go beyond UM authentication

checklist.description: Alerts for unusual login, repeated OTP, unusual QR, or abnormal amounts — triggered at the right moment.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Unusual login patterns trigger alert
• Repeated OTP attempts flagged
• Unusual QR source signaled
• Abnormal amounts (vs. user history) flagged

checklist.description: When a user is flagged or blocked, they have a self-service path to resolve it in-product.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Blocked user shown clear reason
• Self-service resolution path exists in-product
• No dead-ends requiring unprompted CS contact
checklist.failure_example: Agreement pay: risk-rejected transaction. User can't find face authentication entry point from Home or LSGD.

checklist.description: Latency or frozen UI damages trust, especially at payment. Processing state, timeouts, and graceful fallbacks must be present.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Progress/processing state clearly shown
• UI never appears frozen without feedback
• Timeout includes specific message + 'check history' CTA
• Double-tap disabled on submit
• Graceful fallback on weak network

checklist.description: Behavior and copy consistent between iOS and Android on critical flows.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Same terminology/labels across platforms
• Same back/close, OTP, deeplink/callback logic
• No feature gating discrepancy causing different information
• UI follows platform conventions but business logic unchanged
checklist.failure_example: Images occasionally broken on Android; large system font size breaks layout.

checklist.description: Consent collection process is known and meets data privacy standards required by internal and external stakeholders.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Consent flow mapped and documented
• Meets SBV / internal data privacy requirements
• Consent is informed, not buried in T&C
• Re-consent triggered when scope changes

checklist.description: Irreversible actions have clear confirmation, recipient verification, amount confirmation, and typo prevention.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Clear confirmation step before irreversible action
• Recipient and amount confirmed together
• Cooling-off or review step where appropriate
• Typo prevention (e.g., amount field validation)
• OTP does not lead directly to success without final confirmation
checklist.failure_example: Stock purchase: entering OTP went straight to successful order status with no final confirmation screen.

checklist.description: All 'money states' are consistent and explainable — not just balance. Available vs. pending/hold, source-specific availability, history reconciles with debits/credits.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Available vs. pending/hold clearly distinguished
• Source-specific availability shown per payment method
• History matches actual amount debited/credited
• Benefits/points effect on total clearly shown
• Refresh/sync after transaction: no mismatch across home/cashier/result/history
checklist.failure_example: TikTokShop x BNPL: user approved for limit X, attempts purchase with limit X, TikTok says insufficient limit — because risk updated limit without notifying user.

checklist.description: Fees, who pays, waiver conditions, and actual deducted amount shown clearly — not too early (kills motivation) and not too late (feels deceptive).
checklist.what_to_check:
• Fee shown before confirmation, not after
• Who pays the fee is explicit
• Waiver conditions clearly stated
• Actual amount deducted matches what was shown
checklist.failure_example: Installment users merged with BNPL now incur BNPL fees — cashier didn't explain why fee appeared.

checklist.description: Benefits and promotions applied in-flow are transparent. Segment-specific eligibility is clear. Gap between advertised and actual received is minimized.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Benefit application clearly shown in flow
• Segment eligibility explained where relevant
• Edge cases where benefit may not apply are identified
• Guardrails minimize advertised vs. actual gap
checklist.failure_example: Promotion: segment received voucher but couldn't use it.

checklist.description: For non-instant outcomes (fund purchase, IBFT pending, stock, BNPL/CL approval, eKYC): settlement timeline, reconciliation process, and next steps are clear.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Settlement timeline shown clearly
• Reconciliation process explained
• Next steps provided while waiting
• User knows what will happen and when
checklist.failure_example: Fund purchase: user doesn't know when shares are added to portfolio.

checklist.description: Every transaction maps to ledger. Internal vs. partner reconciliation. Settlement mismatch alerts. Stuck transaction detection.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Every transaction has ledger mapping
• Internal/partner reconciliation process defined
• Mismatch alerts configured
• Stuck transaction detection in place

checklist.description: Users understand their transaction limits, daily caps, and KYC tier restrictions before hitting them — with a clear upgrade path.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Current limits visible in-product (not buried in FAQ)
• Limit displayed at entry point for limit-sensitive flows
• When limit is reached: clear explanation + upgrade path
• Daily/monthly cap resets communicated
• KYC tier upgrade path surfaced proactively
checklist.failure_example: User attempts high-value transfer, hits limit mid-flow with no explanation of current limit or how to increase it.

checklist.description: Where SBV regulations require specific disclosures, confirmations, or cooling-off periods, these are designed intentionally — not bolted on in ways that confuse users.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Mandatory disclosures (lending, insurance, securities) are clear and placed correctly
• Cooling-off periods explained in plain language
• Regulatory consent is distinguishable from marketing consent
• Compliance UX reviewed with legal/compliance team
• Regulatory friction is acknowledged and minimized where allowed

checklist.description: Users know what they can do after a transaction goes wrong — dispute, refund, chargeback — and can initiate this in-product.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Dispute initiation path visible from transaction detail
• Refund eligibility and timeline communicated
• For lending: early repayment, restructure, complaint options are discoverable
• User rights not hidden in T&C only
• CS escalation path reachable from transaction detail
checklist.failure_example: User charged incorrectly; transaction detail shows no dispute option — only a generic CS contact button with no context.

checklist.description: Default SoF logic is generalizable across all payment methods. Defaults to most-recently-used + available. User can override and app remembers choice.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Default follows recent behavior + availability
• Avoids defaulting to high-fail sources
• User can override and preference is remembered
• Available/unavailable sources clearly shown
• Changing source doesn't break flow state

checklist.description: Commitment to openness: user can choose any SoF; if not, they understand why; user can configure SoF per product/context.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Any SoF can be selected where technically supported
• Restrictions are explained (not silent)
• User can configure SoF preference per product
• BNPL not limited to ZLP VietQR only
checklist.failure_example: BNPL not yet available for non-ZLP VietQR.

checklist.description: Users can link, unlink, view status, and understand consequences of unlinking.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Link/unlink flow is straightforward
• Current linked status visible
• Consequences of unlinking explained
• Unlinking doesn't break active agreement-pay arrangements silently

checklist.description: When primary source fails: logical fallback suggestion (change source, top up, retry).
checklist.what_to_check:
• Failure message names the failed source
• Fallback options offered (change SoF, top-up, retry)
• Fallback doesn't lose transaction state

checklist.description: Before entering a flow, user understands what they're about to do and has all prerequisites. Clarity ≠ verbosity.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Prerequisites communicated at entry (not mid-flow)
• Clear description of what will happen
• Avoid confusing 'clear' with 'lengthy' — concise is better

checklist.description: Loading, processing, waiting for partner/bank callback — each state is visually distinguishable.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Loading vs. processing vs. waiting for partner clearly differentiated
• Round-trip journey states (outbound/return) shown
• No silent waiting states

checklist.description: Result screen clearly distinguishes: success / fail / pending / under reconciliation. Copy is unambiguous.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Success, fail, pending, reconciliation — visually and copy-wise distinct
• No contradictory signals (e.g., green tick + 'processing' copy)
checklist.failure_example: IBFT result page: 'Transaction processing' copy with green success tick — contradictory signals.

checklist.description: Key events have IDs: transaction ID, ticket number, request ID, application ID.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Transactions have transaction IDs
• OTA/Movie tickets have ticket numbers
• KYC/NFC/bank link requests have request IDs
• Loan/BNPL applications have application IDs
• IDs are copyable from the UI

checklist.description: When service is in maintenance or interrupted: status is clear, scope is accurate, ETA provided, alternatives offered.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Maintenance vs. error: clearly distinguished
• Scope: ZLP issue vs. partner issue
• ETA or expected window shown
• Alternatives offered (different service, retry later)
• Entry point disabled before user reaches cashier
• For permanent sunsetting: entry point removed + migration explanation

checklist.description: Necessary information appears at the right decision moment — not too early, not too late.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Fee/limit/KYC requirement shown before confirm step
• Risk warning shown immediately before high-risk action
• Downtime signaled at entry or before submit
• Benefit/voucher conditions shown at point of use
• Avoid overload: only show what's needed at that step
checklist.failure_example: Lending fee: user told about fee on day 28 but was actually charged from first transaction. Agreement pay: only discovered insufficient funds after Apple already billed.

checklist.description: When displayed data may be cached or outdated (portfolio value, credit limit, loyalty points, exchange rates), the product signals this to the user.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Cached data shows last-updated timestamp or freshness indicator
• High-stakes numbers (balance, limit, portfolio value) have refresh option
• Stale credit limit doesn't mislead users into starting a transaction they can't complete
• Exchange rate validity window shown for FX transactions
checklist.failure_example: Portfolio value on stock home different from portfolio value on FS hub — user confused about true balance.

checklist.description: After a transaction, all affected balances, history entries, and statuses sync promptly and consistently across all surfaces.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Balance updates on home after transaction
• History entry appears within expected time
• Linked bank status reflects latest
• Voucher/ticket status updates
• Loyalty/points balance updates
• No contradictory states between home/cashier/result/history
checklist.failure_example: Voucher used in transaction still shows as 'available' in voucher wallet 10 minutes later.

checklist.description: Errors are specific (PIN/OTP/bank timeout/insufficient funds/KYC/maintenance) not generic.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Error message names the specific issue
• Error code available for CS reference
• Different error types have distinct messages
checklist.failure_example: Risk-blocked transactions show generic 'transaction failed'; MMF rule violations don't explain what rule was violated.

checklist.description: Every error tells the user their next step: retry / change source / check history / contact CS / upgrade KYC.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Next step explicitly stated
• Action button present where appropriate
• Entry point for self-service resolution is reachable
checklist.failure_example: Agreement pay risk-blocked: user needs face auth to re-enable but can't find entry point from Home or LSGD. Stock order not matched: no explanation of why (odd lot = lower match probability).

checklist.description: On failure, retain user input (amount, bill code, recipient, source) to avoid re-entry.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Form input preserved on error/failure
• User can correct and retry without re-entering all fields

checklist.description: Retry / back / refresh doesn't cause double payment or duplicate request. Anti-duplication mechanism in place.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Retry is idempotent
• Back/refresh doesn't resubmit
• Duplicate request detection active

checklist.description: Error UI is not abrupt or alarming. Visual design is calm. Copy is supportive, not accusatory. Inline guidance preferred over full-screen modals for routine errors.
checklist.what_to_check:
• No sudden full-screen error popups for routine failures
• No aggressive red warning graphics for non-critical errors
• Copy is calm, clear, supportive
• Inline guidance used where appropriate instead of modal interruption
checklist.failure_example: Sudden full-screen red warning modal for a routine OTP failure. Copy: 'Transaction FAILED!' with no context.

checklist.description: The product team has intentionally designed what the product looks like during peak load (Tet, 11/11), partner maintenance, or partial outage — not left to chance.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Degraded-mode states are designed and documented
• Entry points disabled or labeled before user reaches cashier
• Degraded-mode tested before peak events
• Rollback / feature flag capability in place
• Product team notified of partner maintenance windows in advance
checklist.failure_example: Tet period: payment feature silently degraded; users discover failure at cashier with no warning.

checklist.description: OTP delivery reliability across carriers is monitored. Fallback OTP mechanisms exist. Failures are surfaced to users with recovery options.
checklist.what_to_check:
• OTP delivery success rate monitored by carrier
• Fallback mechanism available (in-app OTP, alternative contact)
• User shown how to retry or use fallback if OTP not received
• OTP expiry is generous enough for realistic delivery times
• Rate limiting doesn't block legitimate OTP retries
checklist.failure_example: User on certain carrier doesn't receive OTP; no fallback; flow is completely blocked.

checklist.description: When an issue occurs, the support system has enough context to resolve quickly without asking the user to repeat basic information.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Transaction/biller/merchant auto-attached to case
• Status, SoF, and timeline available to agent
• Reference ID and error code present
• User/agent not required to re-provide basic info
checklist.failure_example: User calls CS without reference event ID; CS must trace actions from userID only.

checklist.description: Cases are routed to the correct queue/team with clear ownership. Issue classified by domain (bank/partner/mini-app/transfer).
checklist.what_to_check:
• Issue classification by domain is automated or guided
• SLA defined per category
• Escalation path defined
• Handoff between queues is traced

checklist.description: Handler (user self-serve or agent) knows the 'next best action'. Specific guidance. Guardrail against double-submit.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Specific guidance per error type (retry, wait, refund, contact bank)
• Double-submit guardrail active
• Response templates are consistent and accurate

checklist.description: Case/transaction status is visible and updated. New → in progress → waiting reconciliation → resolved. User and agent understand what's being waited for.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Status stages are well-defined
• User can see current status in-product
• Agent can see full status history
• Clear communication when waiting on external party (bank/partner)

checklist.description: When user reaches a dead-end they cannot self-resolve, there is a pathway to contact CS directly from the product page or status page.
checklist.what_to_check:
• CS contact option on error/dead-end screens
• CS contact pre-populates context (transaction ID, flow)
• Not just a generic 'contact us' link
checklist.failure_example: eKYC NFC flow: user fails, doesn't know what to do, no CS entry point from failure screen.

checklist.description: Identify what is currently handled manually in connection with this product — assess automation opportunity or risk.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Manual processes supporting this product are documented
• Volume and frequency of manual work estimated
• Automation roadmap or risk noted

checklist.description: Speed of home/QR scan/confirm/result/history; performs on weak network and mid-range devices.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Load time benchmarked for top 10 critical screens
• Tested on weak network (3G equivalent)
• Tested on mid-range Android device

checklist.description: After action: balance/history/linked bank/voucher/ticket status syncs in time. No mismatch between surfaces.
checklist.what_to_check:
• All affected surfaces sync after action
• Sync timing is within acceptable window
• No contradictory states across surfaces
checklist.failure_example: Portfolio value on stock home ≠ portfolio value on FS hub.

checklist.description: Flows through bank app / webview / partner return to correct state; session is not lost.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Return from bank app lands on correct state
• Webview return preserves session
• Partner callback handled correctly
• Session not lost on return

checklist.description: Critical flows have reference/debug info. Error codes mappable. Key steps logged. Sensitive data not exposed.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Error/status codes are documented and mappable
• Reference ID available for CS/tech lookup
• Key step logs: submit, callback, processing, result
• Retry-safe vs. not-safe states distinguishable
• No sensitive data exposed in logs/UI

checklist.description: Failure in one feature doesn't cascade to crash home or cashier. Partner/mini-app downtime doesn't block core payment.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Feature failure doesn't crash home/cashier
• Partner/mini-app down doesn't block core payment
• Controlled degradation: entry point hidden/disabled
• Impact scope communicated clearly
• Fallback available
checklist.failure_example: Tarot feature failure caused cascade to transfer product.

checklist.description: Critical actions/APIs have rate limits/cooldowns. OTP, login, payment submission protected. Message shown when limit hit.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Rate limits defined for OTP, login, payment, resend
• Limit is reasonable relative to legitimate behavior
• Message clear when limit hit + wait time shown
• No 'false positive' lockouts without recovery
• Combined with double-submit protection

checklist.description: Unique transaction IDs. Retries are safe. Duplicate submissions prevented. Network retry safe. Webhook duplicates handled.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Unique transaction ID per submission
• Retry of same transaction is idempotent
• Duplicate form submission prevented
• Network-level retry safe
• Webhook/callback duplicates deduplicated

checklist.description: When something that affects the user changes — rates, limits, fees, product terms, partner changes — the user is informed proactively in-product, not discovered by surprise.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Rate changes (MMF yield, lending rate) communicated before effective date
• Limit changes notified before user hits new limit
• Fee structure changes given notice period
• Partner sunset/change communicated with migration path
• Communication is in-product, not just email/T&C update
checklist.failure_example: User discovers their MMF yield rate dropped only when they check their monthly summary — no in-app notification was sent.

checklist.description: The product gets demonstrably better at serving a user the longer they use it: smarter defaults, remembered preferences, proactive relevant alerts.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Defaults improve based on usage history
• Preferences are remembered and applied
• Relevant alerts sent (bill due, low balance before scheduled payment)
• Power users are rewarded with efficiency, not penalized with more friction

checklist.description: A user who had a bad experience (failed transaction, blocked account, support case) has a clear path to confidence recovery — visible in-product.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Post-resolution: user shown what was fixed
• Account restored: clear confirmation in-product
• Long-pending case: proactive status update sent
• User not left to wonder if their issue was actually resolved
checklist.failure_example: User's disputed transaction resolved after 5 days — no in-app notification, user discovers it by checking history.

checklist.description: Users can see the cumulative value they've received from using the product (savings, cashback, interest earned, benefits used) — making the value proposition tangible over time.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Cumulative cashback/rewards visible
• Interest earned shown (SDSL, MMF)
• Benefit summary available (monthly or on demand)
• Value realization tied to specific product actions
• Helps retention by making switching costs tangible

checklist.description: The product communicates in a consistent tone across all touchpoints — in-flow copy, notifications, error messages, marketing banners — that aligns with Zalopay's brand.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Error messages: calm, supportive, not accusatory
• Marketing copy: doesn't oversell or create false urgency
• Notifications: informative, not alarmist
• Financial copy: precise, not vague
• Tone is consistent across iOS, Android, and web

checklist.description: The product does not set expectations it cannot reliably meet. Promises made in onboarding, marketing, or UX copy are consistently fulfilled.
checklist.what_to_check:
• Advertised features work as described
• Processing time estimates are accurate (not aspirational)
• Benefit eligibility matches what was communicated
• Edge cases where promises may not hold are acknowledged
checklist.failure_example: Onboarding promises 'instant transfer to any bank' — user later discovers certain banks have 30-min delays with no in-flow warning.

summary.value: Which product flows / user journeys were reviewed?
List top findings here — dimension, criterion, and specific gap
Notable strengths or minor polish items
Blockers or items needing input from other teams
